title: WRF (Weather Research and Forecasting) Model
description: The Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) Model is a next-generation mesoscale numerical weather prediction system designed for both atmospheric research and operational forecasting. It was developed collaboratively by NCAR, NOAA, and numerous research organizations over several decades and is now at version 4.7. WRF supports a wide range of meteorological applications, from idealized research simulations to real-data regional weather forecasting and climate studies. The model's primary dynamical core is the Advanced Research WRF (ARW) solver, which uses an Eulerian mass-coordinate formulation on an Arakawa C-grid with Runge-Kutta time integration. WRF offers a comprehensive suite of interchangeable physics parameterization packages covering microphysics, cumulus convection, planetary boundary layer, radiation, and land-surface processes. The software is built around a strict, three-layer hierarchical architecture that separates the Driver Layer (parallelism, I/O, domain management) from the Mediation Layer (solver orchestration) and the Model Layer (physical equations). A central Active Data Registry system defines all model state variables, I/O streams, nesting behavior, and namelist parameters in a single text file, from which build-time code-generation tools produce the corresponding Fortran routines. WRF supports multi-level parallelism with shared-memory OpenMP, distributed-memory MPI (via the RSL_LITE communication layer), and hybrid modes, scaling to tens of thousands of processors. The model includes sophisticated domain nesting capabilities, including two-way interactive, one-way, and automatically moving nests for hurricane tracking. An extensive I/O API abstracts multiple file formats, including NetCDF, parallel NetCDF, HDF5, GRIB1, GRIB2, and binary, and supports asynchronous quilting I/O servers. WRF-Chem is an optional module that fully couples gas-phase chemistry, aerosol microphysics, photolysis, emissions, and deposition within the forecast model. The WRFDA subsystem provides a complete 3D-Var and 4D-Var data assimilation framework capable of ingesting dozens of observation types including satellites, radar, and conventional data. WRF-Hydro extends the modeling system with a fully coupled terrestrial hydrological model that handles overland flow, channel routing, and baseflow. A tangent linear and adjoint version of the model (WRFPLUS) enables ensemble Kalman filter methods and gradient-based sensitivity analysis. The build system uses a combination of Perl and shell configure scripts together with CMake, and a suite of C-based Registry code generators that produce domain data structures and communication stubs from the Registry specification files.
